name: Bulgarian Split Squat
resistance_profile: Rear-foot-elevated single-leg squat with a big glute/quad stretch.
execution_steps: Stand a stride in front of a bench and rest the top of one foot behind you on it.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Lower straight down until your front thigh reaches a deep stretch, torso leaning slightly forward.
Drive through the front heel to stand back up without pushing off the rear foot.
Finish all reps on one leg, then switch.
good_when: You want a big quad and glute stretch stimulus with modest spinal load.
You're fixing left/right imbalances or training around a cranky lower back.
You have only dumbbells at home or in a limited setup.
bad_when: Poor balance makes it hard to load or reach depth (use a leg press or supported split squat).
You want maximum absolute load with minimal stabilization demand.
